sections.title: While finding a book for them

lines.speaker: Professor Gracklebone
lines.text: Have you found my book, human? '[book name]'

lines.speaker: Professor Gracklebone
lines.text: If you do, I bet the Arceuus citizens will be grateful for you helping their customers. They must know their Librarian's useless.

sections.title: While finding a book for someone else

lines.speaker: Professor Gracklebone
lines.text: Aren't you helping someone else at the moment? Don't let me interrupt.

lines.speaker: Professor Gracklebone
lines.text: Thanks, human. You can have this other book that I don't want.

sections.title: Talking to them after giving them the book

lines.speaker: Professor Gracklebone
lines.text: Thanks for finding my book. I'm learning such a lot here.

lines.speaker: Professor Gracklebone
lines.text: Hey, human, can you do me a favour? That Librarian won't help me find a book.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, what book do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Professor Gracklebone
lines.text: I'm from the Stronghold. Mostly I teach magic, and stuff like that. Anyway, I heard they'd got magic here unlike anything I'd ever seen, so I came to learn what I could.

lines.speaker: Professor Gracklebone
lines.text: It'd really help with my learning if you'd find my book for me. Will you?
